title: Missing default case in value type switch allows invalid CBOR
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c
Lines: 225-244

CATEGORY: CORRECTNESS

The switch statement on rec->type has cases for SENML_VALUE_FLOAT, SENML_VALUE_BOOL, SENML_VALUE_STRING, and SENML_VALUE_DATA, but no default case.

If rec->type contains an invalid value (memory corruption, uninitialized struct, API misuse), the switch falls through without error. The function will:
1. Start a CBOR map expecting a value entry
2. Never encode the value
3. Successfully end the map

This produces a malformed SenML record - a CBOR map that promised N entries but only has N-1.

IMPACT:
- Malformed CBOR output
- RFC 8428 violation (record must have a value)
- Silent failure - no error returned

FIX: Add default case that returns -EINVAL or -ENOTSUP for unknown value types:
  default:
      return -EINVAL;
